Task: Lock contest scope and success criteria
Acceptance Criteria: One-sentence goal, non-goals, budget cap, and ship checklist are written before coding.
Risk Notes: Prevents scope creep into Zoom/native/true AR.

Acceptance Criteria: No implementation work goes to native plugins, virtual camera, or depth occlusion for contest MVP.
Risk Notes: This is how the project blows up.

Task: Create GitHub repo and Next.js app scaffold
Acceptance Criteria: Repo runs locally with npm install + npm run dev; deployed placeholder page works.
Risk Notes: Hidden token cost from repo scans; keep agent prompts scoped.

Acceptance Criteria: OPENAI_API_KEY documented; key never exposed client-side.
Risk Notes: Security failure if API key leaks to browser.

Task: Build camera capture and demo-scene fallback
Acceptance Criteria: Use My Camera and Try Demo Scene both work; denial routes to sample mode without dead end.
Risk Notes: Camera permission failure is a judge-killer unless fallback is instant.

Acceptance Criteria: POST /api/analyze returns validated JSON: scene, lighting, mood, attachment points, character concept, image prompt, placement, caption.
Risk Notes: Malformed JSON must fall back safely; no chain-of-thought display.

Acceptance Criteria: UI visibly shows GPT-5.5 scene summary, mood, placement logic, companion concept, and generated caption.
Risk Notes: Without this it looks like a sticker generator.

Acceptance Criteria: POST /api/generate returns one companion image from GPT-5.5-created prompt; errors show fallback.
Risk Notes: Image latency/cost risk; no public unlimited generations.

Acceptance Criteria: User can drag, scale, rotate, and reset companion over snapshot/demo scene.
Risk Notes: Bad transform UX makes demo feel broken.

Acceptance Criteria: Companion has soft shadow, slight scene tint/opacity controls, and fallback sticker-frame mode.
Risk Notes: Main failure mode: pasted PNG effect.

Acceptance Criteria: Exported PNG includes scene, companion, companion name, caption, and #OpenAIDevDay2026 footer.
Risk Notes: Share card is the viral/judge artifact.

Task: Add session/IP rate limit and cost gate
Acceptance Criteria: One generation/session in browser and server-side abuse guard active before public posting.
Risk Notes: No public link without this.

Task: Add companion mode selector: 3 modes only
Acceptance Criteria: Desk Critter, Keyboard Pet, Floating Familiar alter the prompt and UI; no overloaded options.
Risk Notes: Too many modes creates confusion.

Acceptance Criteria: Subtle bob/glow/shadow animation that does not block export or placement.
Risk Notes: Polish only after core loop is stable.

Acceptance Criteria: Docs explain 10-second pitch, GPT-5.5 role, Image Gen role, quick test instructions, and limitations.
Risk Notes: Docs help human and machine review.

Acceptance Criteria: Clearly states snapshots are sent only on action, not stored by app, and generation is capped.
Risk Notes: Privacy concern from webcam is predictable; answer it upfront.

Task: Deploy to Vercel and validate HTTPS
Acceptance Criteria: Public link works on desktop; camera prompt appears; demo mode works without camera; API env vars set.
Risk Notes: getUserMedia requires secure context outside localhost.

Acceptance Criteria: Video shows open link → snap/demo → readout → generated companion → drag/export.
Risk Notes: People may not click; video raises odds.

Acceptance Criteria: Post includes #OpenAIDevDay2026, playable link, repo/build notes, and one-line built-with explanation.
Risk Notes: Submission format matters.

Task: Final smoke test from fresh browser
Acceptance Criteria: Test incognito: no login, camera denied path, demo path, successful generation, export card, mobile layout sanity.
Risk Notes: Judge experience must not depend on dev machine state.
